skill.name: skill-judge
skill.description: Evaluate Agent Skill design quality against official specifications and best practices. Use it to review SKILL.md files, score skill packages, find token waste, and suggest concrete improvements.
skill.summary: Score and improve Agent Skills with a structured quality rubric.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found them to be false positives from Markdown code fences, inline examples, and evaluation prose. No executable scripts, real cryptographic API usage, network calls, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration behavior were found in README.md or SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command detections occur in documentation examples and fenced Markdown blocks, such as sample prompts and illustrative workflows. They are not executable scripts and do not take user input for shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown examples, not runnable code files. No shell wrapper, command dispatcher, or dynamic command construction was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Hits
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts are not supported by the reviewed content. The flagged areas are skill descriptions, scoring criteria, and prose about skill evaluation rather than MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown guidance documents and do not contain cryptographic implementation code. The static category appears to match ordinary prose tokens rather than security-sensitive APIs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network-related static hit does not correspond to a real HTTP client, socket call, scan, or external endpoint. The reviewed content discusses AI skill context and tool concepts only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no request library use, URL, destination host, or data transfer operation. The only relevant text is conceptual documentation.

content.value_statement: Many Agent Skills waste context on content the model already knows. Skill Judge gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured rubric for scoring SKILL.md files and improving them.
actual_capabilities: Scores Agent Skills across eight documented quality dimensions.
Classifies content as expert knowledge, activation reminders, or redundant guidance.
Checks SKILL.md descriptions for activation triggers and searchable keywords.
Identifies common skill design failures such as tutorials, vague warnings, and overlong files.
Produces prioritized improvement suggestions for skill authors.
Supports comparison of multiple skill packages using one rubric.
limitations: It is a review framework, not an automated validator or package scanner.
It does not execute tests or verify referenced scripts by itself.
Scores depend on the model reading the full skill content carefully.
It focuses on design quality, not legal licensing or malware detection.

use_cases.title: Pre-Publication Skill Review
use_cases.description: Review a submitted SKILL.md before publishing and identify issues that reduce activation, usability, or knowledge value.

use_cases.title: Author Improvement Pass
use_cases.description: Turn a draft skill into a clearer, more useful package with prioritized edits and a score baseline.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Quality Comparison
use_cases.description: Compare several internal skills with the same rubric and decide which ones need redesign first.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single SKILL.md file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this SKILL.md with Skill Judge. Give me the total score, grade, main issues, and top three improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Knowledge Delta
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused pass on redundant or obvious content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Skill Judge to classify each section as expert, activation, or redundant. Tell me what to delete, compress, or keep.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two skill packages with the Skill Judge rubric. Explain which one is stronger and why.

prompt_templates.title: Marketplace Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced publication review with action plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill for marketplace readiness. Score every dimension, identify publication blockers, and create a prioritized remediation plan.

output_examples.input: Review a draft skill for creating spreadsheets.
output_examples.output: A scored report with dimension ratings, a knowledge ratio, activation issues, and three concrete edits to improve spreadsheet-specific guidance.

output_examples.input: Check whether a skill is too long and tutorial-like.
output_examples.output: A section-by-section classification that separates expert guidance from redundant explanations and recommends what to move or remove.

output_examples.input: Compare two internal skills before deployment.
output_examples.output: A side-by-side quality assessment with grades, pattern fit, usability risks, and a recommendation for which skill to revise first.

best_practices: Provide the full SKILL.md and any referenced files when asking for a review.
Ask for evidence-based scoring with line references and concrete improvement steps.
Use the first report as a baseline, then rerun the rubric after revisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the score alone without reading the cited reasons.
Do not ask it to judge security risk as a replacement for a dedicated security audit.
Do not review only the README when the actual SKILL.md is available.

faq.question: What does Skill Judge evaluate?
faq.answer: It evaluates Agent Skill design quality, including knowledge value, structure, activation, anti-patterns, and practical usability.

faq.question: Can it improve my SKILL.md?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify weak sections and suggest specific edits to improve clarity, activation, and usefulness.

faq.question: Does it run code or install dependencies?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown-based review framework and does not require external tools.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude Code and Codex users?
faq.answer: Yes. The rubric applies to skills intended for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Can it compare multiple skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It can score multiple skills with one rubric and explain their relative strengths and weaknesses.

faq.question: Does it replace human judgment?
faq.answer: No. It structures the review, but humans should verify scores, evidence, and publication decisions.
